en: And David the king said to all the assembly, "Solomon my son, whom alone God has chosen, is young and inexperienced, and the work is great, for the palace will not be for man but for the LORD God.
zh: 大衛王對全體會眾說：“我的兒子所羅門，是 神所揀選的，現在還年幼識淺，而這工程非常浩大；因為這殿宇不是為人建造，而是為耶和華 神建造的。

en: So I have provided for the house of my God, so far as I was able, the gold for the things of gold, the silver for the things of silver, and the bronze for the things of bronze, the iron for the things of iron, and wood for the things of wood, besides great quantities of onyx and stones for setting, antimony, colored stones, all sorts of precious stones and marble.
zh: 我為我 神的殿已經盡了我的力量，預備了金子做金器，銀子做銀器，銅做銅器，鐵做鐵器，木做木器；還有紅瑪瑙、用作鑲嵌的寶石、彩石和各樣的寶石，以及很多大理石。

en: Moreover, in addition to all that I have provided for the holy house, I have a treasure of my own of gold and silver, and because of my devotion to the house of my God I give it to the house of my God:
zh: 不但這樣，因我愛慕我 神的殿，就在我為建造聖殿預備的一切以外，又把我自己積蓄的金銀獻給我 神的殿：

en: 3,000 talents of gold, of the gold of Ophir, and 7,000 talents of refined silver, for overlaying the walls of the house,
zh: 就是俄斐金一百多公噸，精煉的銀子約兩百四十公噸，用來貼殿裡的牆壁；

en: and for all the work to be done by craftsmen, gold for the things of gold and silver for the things of silver. Who then will offer willingly, consecrating himself today to the LORD?"
zh: 金子做金器，銀子做銀器，以及匠人手所作的各樣巧工。今日有誰願意奉獻給耶和華呢？”

en: Then the leaders of fathers' houses made their freewill offerings, as did also the leaders of the tribes, the commanders of thousands and of hundreds, and the officers over the king's work.
zh: 於是各家族的領袖、以色列各支派的領袖、千夫長、百夫長和管理王的事務的領袖，都樂意奉獻。

en: They gave for the service of the house of God 5,000 talents and 10,000 darics of gold, 10,000 talents of silver, 18,000 talents of bronze and 100,000 talents of iron.
zh: 為了神殿裡的需用，他們奉獻了金子一百七十多公噸，銀子三百四十多公噸，銅約六百二十公噸和鐵三千四百公噸。

en: And whoever had precious stones gave them to the treasury of the house of the LORD, in the care of Jehiel the Gershonite.
zh: 有寶石的，都交到耶和華殿這庫房，由革順人耶歇經辦。

en: Then the people rejoiced because they had given willingly, for with a whole heart they had offered freely to the LORD. David the king also rejoiced greatly.
zh: 人民因這些人自願奉獻而歡喜，因為他們一心樂意奉獻給耶和華，大衛王也非常歡喜。

en: Therefore David blessed the LORD in the presence of all the assembly. And David said: "Blessed are you, O LORD, the God of Israel our father, forever and ever.
zh: 所以大衛在全體會眾面前稱頌耶和華，說：“耶和華我們的祖先以色列的 神，是應當稱頌的，從亙古直到永遠。

en: Yours, O LORD, is the greatness and the power and the glory and the victory and the majesty, for all that is in the heavens and in the earth is yours. Yours is the kingdom, O LORD, and you are exalted as head above all.
zh: 耶和華啊，尊大、能力、榮耀、勝利和威嚴，都是你的；因為天上地下的萬有都是你的；耶和華啊，國度是你的，你是至高的，是萬有之首。

en: Both riches and honor come from you, and you rule over all. In your hand are power and might, and in your hand it is to make great and to give strength to all.
zh: 富足和尊榮都從你而來，你也統治萬有。在你的手裡有力量和權能；人的尊大、強盛都是出於你的手。

en: And now we thank you, our God, and praise your glorious name.
zh: 我們的 神啊，現在我們要稱頌你，讚美你榮耀的名。

en: "But who am I, and what is my people, that we should be able thus to offer willingly? For all things come from you, and of your own have we given you.
zh: 我算甚麼？我的人民又算甚麼？竟有力量這樣樂意奉獻？因為萬物都是從你而來，我們只是把從你手裡得來的，奉獻給你。

en: For we are strangers before you and sojourners, as all our fathers were. Our days on the earth are like a shadow, and there is no abiding.
zh: 我們在你面前是客旅，是寄居的，像我們的列祖一樣；我們在世上的日子好像影兒，沒有指望。

en: O LORD our God, all this abundance that we have provided for building you a house for your holy name comes from your hand and is all your own.
zh: 耶和華我們的 神啊，我們預備的這一切財物，要為你的聖名建造殿宇，都是從你而來的，也都是屬於你的。

en: I know, my God, that you test the heart and have pleasure in uprightness. In the uprightness of my heart I have freely offered all these things, and now I have seen your people, who are present here, offering freely and joyously to you.
zh: 我的 神啊，我知道你察驗人心，喜悅正直；至於我，我以正直的心甘願奉獻這一切；現在我也非常歡喜看見你在這裡的人民，都甘願奉獻給你。

en: O LORD, the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Israel, our fathers, keep forever such purposes and thoughts in the hearts of your people, and direct their hearts toward you.
zh: 耶和華我們列祖亞伯拉罕、以撒、以色列的 神啊，求你永遠保守這一件事，就是使你的子民常存這心思意念，堅定他們的心歸向你。

en: Grant to Solomon my son a whole heart that he may keep your commandments, your testimonies, and your statutes, performing all, and that he may build the palace for which I have made provision."
zh: 求你賜給我的兒子所羅門專一的心，謹守你的誡命、法度和律例，作成這一切事，用我所預備的一切來建造殿宇。”

en: Then David said to all the assembly, "Bless the LORD your God." And all the assembly blessed the LORD, the God of their fathers, and bowed their heads and paid homage to the LORD and to the king.
zh: 大衛對全體會眾說：“你們應當稱頌耶和華你們的 神。”於是全體會眾就稱頌耶和華他們列祖的 神，俯伏敬拜耶和華和王。

en: And they offered sacrifices to the LORD, and on the next day offered burnt offerings to the LORD, 1,000 bulls, 1,000 rams, and 1,000 lambs, with their drink offerings, and sacrifices in abundance for all Israel.
zh: 第二天，他們向耶和華獻祭，他們向耶和華獻上燔祭；那一天他們獻了公牛一千頭、公綿羊一千隻、羊羔一千隻，以及同獻的奠祭，又為以色列眾人獻了許多祭。

en: And they ate and drank before the LORD on that day with great gladness. And they made Solomon the son of David king the second time, and they anointed him as prince for the LORD, and Zadok as priest.
zh: 那一天，他們十分喜樂地在耶和華面前吃喝。他們再次表示擁立大衛的兒子所羅門作王，膏他為耶和華作君王，又膏撒督作祭司。

en: Then Solomon sat on the throne of the LORD as king in place of David his father. And he prospered, and all Israel obeyed him.
zh: 於是所羅門坐在耶和華所賜的位上，接續他父親大衛作王；他凡事亨通，以色列眾人都聽從他。

en: All the leaders and the mighty men, and also all the sons of King David, pledged their allegiance to King Solomon.
zh: 眾領袖和勇士，以及大衛王的眾子，都順服所羅門王。

en: And the LORD made Solomon very great in the sight of all Israel and bestowed on him such royal majesty as had not been on any king before him in Israel.
zh: 耶和華使所羅門在以色列眾人面前非常尊大，又賜給他君王的威嚴，勝過在他以前所有的以色列王。

en: Thus David the son of Jesse reigned over all Israel.
zh: 耶西的兒子大衛作全以色列的王。

en: The time that he reigned over Israel was forty years. He reigned seven years in Hebron and thirty-three years in Jerusalem.
zh: 大衛作王統治以色列的日子共四十年；他在希伯崙作王七年，在耶路撒冷作王三十三年。

en: Then he died at a good age, full of days, riches, and honor. And Solomon his son reigned in his place.
zh: 大衛年紀老邁，壽數滿足，享盡富足和尊榮才去世；他的兒子所羅門接續他作王。

en: Now the acts of King David, from first to last, are written in the Chronicles of Samuel the seer, and in the Chronicles of Nathan the prophet, and in the Chronicles of Gad the seer,
zh: 大衛王一生始末的事蹟，都記在撒母耳先見的書上、拿單先知的書上和迦得先見的書上。

en: with accounts of all his rule and his might and of the circumstances that came upon him and upon Israel and upon all the kingdoms of the countries.
zh: 還有他治理的一切國事，他的英勇事蹟，以及在他身上和以色列並各地列國發生的事蹟，都記在這些書上。
